to verify the CPU TEE that hosts the co: to verify the CPU TEE that hosts the confidential GPU.
A 200 response (for example \"success\"): A 200 response (for example "success") confirms attestation. Connection refused or a policy error means attestation has not completed — check the Trustee logs and the RVPS reference values (PCR8).
A name for this Trustee deployment (the: A name for this Trustee deployment (the TrusteeConfig resource).
Also stored in the {{cm}} ConfigMap as a: Also stored in the {{cm}} ConfigMap as a durable record. Hand the downloaded file to auditors.
Attestation happens inside the confident: Attestation happens inside the confidential VM, so the definitive check runs from a terminal. Fetch a resource from the Confidential Data Hub — a successful response means the TEE was attested by Trustee and the secret was released:
Attestation rules for confidential GPU e: Attestation rules for confidential GPU evidence.
Authorizes which secrets an attested cli: Authorizes which secrets an attested client may retrieve after attestation.
Comma-separated TEE XFAM features to all: Comma-separated TEE XFAM features to allow. veritas warns that without these the default attestation policy’s xfam check fails. Edit to match your hardware.
Confidential workloads in other clusters: Confidential workloads in other clusters attest to this Trustee at:
Could not read the cluster pull secret (: Could not read the cluster pull secret ({{name}} in {{ns}}). veritas needs it to pull the OpenShift release image. This requires cluster-admin, or provide a pull secret in this namespace. ({{detail}})
Could not read the KBS log: Could not read the KBS log
Create secret adds an Opaque Secret and: Create secret adds an Opaque Secret and registers its name on the KbsConfig spec.kbsSecretResources so the operator brokers it after attestation. Use this for attestation-status, image-signature (img-sig), and security-policy resources.
Created as an Opaque Secret in namespace: Created as an Opaque Secret in namespace "{{namespace}}".
Deploy Trustee to start attesting confid: Deploy Trustee to start attesting confidential workloads — one TrusteeConfig generates the KBS, policies, reference values, and secrets.
Expected measurement values the Referenc: Expected measurement values the Reference Value Provider Service checks against TEE evidence — including the PCR8 hash produced by the initdata builder.
Generate a self-signed cert for the KBS: Generate a self-signed cert for the KBS and load it as the TLS secret below (substitute your own CA for production):
GPU attestation depends on services and: GPU attestation depends on services and access outside this cluster:
GPU attestation policy — configure the r: GPU attestation policy — configure the rules NRAS evidence is checked against on the
How the Key Broker Service is reachable. ClusterIP keeps it in-cluster; use NodePort or LoadBalancer to reach it from a separate (hub-and-spoke) cluster.
How to generate this TLS secret: How to generate this TLS secret
HTTP response headers indicate success. A failure usually means an egress firewall is blocking nras.attestation.nvidia.com.
Image-signature keys — produce signing k: Image-signature keys — produce signing keys with Red Hat Trusted Artifact Signer and add them on the Delivered secrets tab.
In remote mode, Trustee must reach NVIDI: In remote mode, Trustee must reach NVIDIA NRAS over egress HTTPS. Confirm connectivity from the Trustee pod:
Intel TDX (on the workload cluster) — re: Intel TDX (on the workload cluster) — retrieve a PCCS API key from the
Intel TDX host — obtain a PCCS API key f: Intel TDX host — obtain a PCCS API key from the
Live attestation activity from the Trust: Live attestation activity from the Trustee KBS log
Name: Name
No attestation activity in the recent lo: No attestation activity in the recent log. Attestation happens when a confidential workload boots and fetches a resource from Trustee.
No attestation reference values are regi: No attestation reference values are registered
No confidential workloads found in this: No confidential workloads found in this cluster yet.
No confidential workloads found in this cluster. Deploy a pod on the kata-cc runtime to see its attestation status here.
No KBS pods found. The Trustee operator: No KBS pods found. The Trustee operator may still be reconciling the KBS.
No secret resources are configured for d: No secret resources are configured for delivery yet.
No secrets generated yet — they appear o: No secrets generated yet — they appear once the operator reconciles the TrusteeConfig.
No warning or attestation-related events: No warning or attestation-related events for this pod.
NVIDIA GPU attestation — requires an NRA: NVIDIA GPU attestation — requires an NRAS licensing agreement and outbound HTTPS to
NVIDIA NRAS — remote GPU attestation req: NVIDIA NRAS — remote GPU attestation requires an NVIDIA Remote Attestation Service (NRAS) licensing agreement and outbound HTTPS egress to
One key=value per line. Values are store: One key=value per line. Values are stored via stringData (the API server base64-encodes them).
One Trustee can attest workloads across: One Trustee can attest workloads across many clusters (hub-and-spoke). This view shows the workloads in the current cluster live; remote spoke clusters attest over the network to the same KBS endpoint shown below. Trustee and confidential containers may also run in the same cluster — then this cluster is both hub and spoke.
Optional TLS secret used to verify attes: Optional TLS secret used to verify attestation tokens issued by KBS.
Parsed from the KBS container log: calls: Parsed from the KBS container log: calls to the KBS API (/kbs/…) and attestation-service events; unrelated traffic is filtered out. This is a cluster-level activity view, not a per-workload cryptographic proof — for that, use the evidence in Attestation status.
paste the initdata.toml from the coco pl: paste the initdata.toml from the coco plugin's Initdata builder
Permissive accepts most attestation evid: Permissive accepts most attestation evidence — good for getting started in dev and test.
Pods labelled app=kbs in namespace \"{{n: Pods labelled app=kbs in namespace "{{namespace}}". Open a pod to view its logs.
Prefer to run veritas outside the cluste: Prefer to run veritas outside the cluster? Generate the ConfigMap manually and import it below.
Reference values — run the veritas tool: Reference values — run the veritas tool from the coco-tools image to produce RVPS reference values, then import them on the Reference values tab.
Reference values must be generated per T: Reference values must be generated per TEE platform
reference-values.json is empty by defaul: reference-values.json is empty by default. Generate it for your TEE platform with the veritas tool, then re-import it here. Regenerate and re-apply it after any TEE firmware, OpenShift, or OpenShift sandboxed containers (OSC) upgrade — those change the expected measurements.
Restricted enforces strict attestation p: Restricted enforces strict attestation policies and requires TLS — use it for production.
Restricted profile — generate an HTTPS T: Restricted profile — generate an HTTPS TLS cert + key (for example with openssl) and load it as the TLS secret below.
Rules the Attestation Service applies to: Rules the Attestation Service applies to CPU TEE evidence (Intel TDX / AMD SEV-SNP).
Runs an in-cluster Job that execs {{pod}: Runs an in-cluster Job that execs {{pod}} and queries its in-guest Confidential Data Hub. A released resource proves the TEE was attested by Trustee. The Job records a signed-by-time evidence document (workload identity, initdata digest, Trustee endpoint, result) you can download for auditors. Note: it requires the workload’s Kata agent policy to permit exec (ExecProcessRequest); if it doesn’t, the result is "inconclusive" and the config-side evidence is still captured.
Select the Trustee hub, a node, or a wor: Select the Trustee hub, a node, or a workload to open it.
Some attestation setup happens outside t: Some attestation setup happens outside the cluster:
The attestation topology shows the confi: The attestation topology shows the confidential workloads a Trustee attests, grouped by the node and cluster they run in. Create a TrusteeConfig to deploy Trustee, then return here.
The cluster pull secret {{name}} has no: The cluster pull secret {{name}} has no .dockerconfigjson key; cannot pull the OpenShift release image.
The Confidential Data Hub returned an er: The Confidential Data Hub returned an error — attestation did not succeed for this resource.
The ConfigMap \"{{name}}\" was not found: The ConfigMap "{{name}}" was not found in namespace "{{namespace}}". It is generated by the Trustee operator from the TrusteeConfig.
The container to exec. It must have curl: The container to exec. It must have curl on its PATH.
The GPU hardware model (for example, H10: The GPU hardware model (for example, H100).
The initdata.toml whose PCR8 measurement: The initdata.toml whose PCR8 measurement is folded into the reference values. Produce it with the Initdata builder in the confidential containers plugin.
The Job execs the pod and queries the Co: The Job execs the pod and queries the Confidential Data Hub. This is quick.
The KBS resource the guest requests, as: The KBS resource the guest requests, as repository/type/tag. A resource only releases after a successful attestation.
The OpenShift release whose extensions i: The OpenShift release whose extensions image veritas pulls to compute measurements. Defaulted from the cluster version.
The probe could not exec the guest (ofte: The probe could not exec the guest (often the agent policy denies exec). The config-side evidence below is still recorded.
The probe Job failed before writing evid: The probe Job failed before writing evidence. Open the Job to view its logs.
The veritas Job failed. Open the Job to: The veritas Job failed. Open the Job to view its pod logs — common causes are an invalid initdata.toml, an unreachable OpenShift release image, or a pull-secret problem.
The veritas Job is running. This pulls t: The veritas Job is running. This pulls the OpenShift release image and computes measurements; it can take a few minutes.
There is already a TrusteeConfig on this: There is already a TrusteeConfig on this cluster. Manage it from the Trustee overview, or create another below.
This is the single resource you are crea: This is the single resource you are creating. The operator reconciles everything else from it.
This runs the veritas tool in an in-clus: This runs the veritas tool in an in-cluster Job (using the cluster pull secret to fetch the OpenShift release image) and writes the result into the {{cm}} ConfigMap. It can take a few minutes. The Reference values editor below reflects the result once the Job succeeds.
TLS secret that terminates HTTPS on the: TLS secret that terminates HTTPS on the KBS endpoint. Required for the Restricted profile.
Trustee is already deployed on this clus: Trustee is already deployed on this cluster
Trustee is the attestation hub. Its Key: Trustee is the attestation hub. Its Key Broker Service (KBS) verifies a confidential workload’s hardware evidence at boot and only then releases secrets and keys to it. Each workload runs inside a node’s Trusted Execution Environment, in a cluster — shown here nested as workload → node → cluster.
Trustee is the Red Hat build of the conf: Trustee is the Red Hat build of the confidential containers attestation service. From this single resource, the operator deploys the Key Broker Service (KBS) and its attestation and resource policies, reference values, and secrets. Your confidential workloads then attest to Trustee when they boot and, if their TEE evidence is trusted, receive their sealed secrets.
Trustee KBS is not ready — confidential: Trustee KBS is not ready — confidential workloads cannot attest
Trustee rejects attestation until refere: Trustee rejects attestation until reference values (RVPS) are registered. Generate them from a TrusteeConfig, or add the PCR8 a workload’s initdata produces.
Trustee released the requested resource: Trustee released the requested resource — the workload is attested.
veritas finished and the measurements we: veritas finished and the measurements were written into {{cm}}. The Reference values editor below now reflects them.
Where the Key Broker Service and its sec: Where the Key Broker Service and its secrets are created. Defaults to the Trustee operator namespace.
Whether NRAS successfully verified the G: Whether NRAS successfully verified the GPU attestation evidence.
Whether the GPU has Confidential Computi: Whether the GPU has Confidential Computing enabled.
You can also manage delivery from the Kb: You can also manage delivery from the KbsConfig
Your GPU attestation policy should valid: Your GPU attestation policy should validate the claims NRAS returns:
